title: Why Jordan Is Becoming a New Focus in the U.S.-Iran War
summary: Four Iranian attacks in five days have targeted U.S. troops in Jordan, U.S. officials said, including one on Friday that killed two soldiers and left another service member missing.

title: U.S. Strikes Leave Iranians Isolated and Scared
summary: Overnight strikes damaged a tunnel and three bridges in Hormozgan Province, the governor’s office said.

title: Zohran Mamdani Knows He Has Political Capital. And He Intends to Spend It.
summary: Lulu Garcia-Navarro sits down for an interview with the mayor of New York City.

title: Mamdani Says He May Still Order Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu’s Arrest in NYC
summary: Mayor Zohran Mamdani said in an interview with The New York Times that he was in “an active conversation” with New York City’s Law Department on whether he had the authority to arrest the Israeli leader.

title: Without Platner, Maine Democrats Scramble to Keep Grassroots Energy Alive
summary: Graham Platner’s campaign for Senate imploded last week. The activists who backed him are seeking a candidate to carry his populist message in the race against Republican Susan Collins.

title: Mike Lindell Not Registered to Vote in Minnesota, Where He is Running for Governor
summary: President Trump endorsed Mike Lindell, a leader of the election denial movement, for Minnesota governor this week.

title: Cindy Burbank, Nebraska’s Democratic Senate Nominee, Files to Withdraw From Race
summary: The move by Cindy Burbank could clear a path for Dan Osborn, an independent candidate backed by her party’s leadership. But Republicans have cried foul, and her effort could face obstacles.

title: A Very British CPAC: Few, if Any, Costumes and Not Much Trump
summary: London hosted an understated version of the Conservative Political Action Conference, known in the United States for its over-the-top celebration of MAGA causes.

title: Has the World Cup Turned Me Into … a Sports Guy?
summary: The pleasures of watching the TV show of the summer as a bandwagon fan.

title: As Trump Scraps With Meloni, His Envoy to Italy Is at Sea
summary: Tilman J. Fertitta, the U.S. ambassador to Rome, is summering on his superyacht as President Trump squabbles with Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni of Italy.

title: The Evolution of Presidential Travel: Horse Carriages to Air Force One
summary: From horse-drawn presidential coaches to Air Force One, presidents have used various modes of transportation to project an image of America for nearly 250 years.

title: Boosie Lobbied for a Trump Pardon. It Never Came. He Wants a Refund.
summary: The Southern rapper paid $600,000 to two right-wing operatives. Now, they’re in a dispute over a contract the rapper believes entitles him to at least half his money back.

title: Iqaluit, Canada’s Most Northern City, Gets Its First Bus Line
summary: Jacinto Marques left Angola for Canada’s frozen edge. Now he’s running a bus service in Iqaluit, the largest, and only city in Canada’s Arctic.

title: Thunderstorms, Flooding and Strong Winds Sweep the Northeast
summary: Forecasters warned of winds over 70 miles per hour in New York City, Philadelphia, Baltimore and Washington.

title: Wildfires Continue To Burn in Ontario, Canada, Sending Smoke to U.S. Cities
summary: Nearly 200 fires continued to devastate swaths of the province as smoke continued to drift across parts of Canada and the United States.

title: What to Know About Fires Caused by Lightning in the Pacific Northwest
summary: Dozens of fires ignited in Oregon and Washington as more than 14,000 lightning strikes hit the region during thunderstorms this week.

title: The Latest Texas Floods Tested Warning Systems. This Time, They Passed.
summary: A lack of sirens and flood forecasting systems made the state’s Hill Country vulnerable to deadly floods last summer. That has changed.

title: Low-T, High Chair
summary: Where are Hegseth’s hormones when Trump needs them?

title: Taylor Farms Expands Iceberg Lettuce Recall to 27 States Amid Cyclospora Outbreak
summary: The F.D.A. said that a new sample from the company’s Mexico facility had tested positive for cyclospora and that the agency was trying to trace affected products.
